title: The Overgrown Gates
lines: The cyclopean steel gates of the city zoo loom out of the Stygian shadows, their once-proud archway now strangled by thick, bioluminescent vines that pulse with a rhythmic, sickly green light—a slow, biological strobe that illuminates the decay.

A flickering neon-green terminal kiosk, caked in crystalline frost and strange, organic slime, greets you at the turnstile. Its screen depicts a static-distorted welcome from a civilization that has been erased from the memory of the stars.

Beyond the threshold, the silence of the dead city is swallowed by a high-frequency, electronic hum and the distant, impossible roars of beasts that should not exist in this or any other reality. The air tastes of ozone and rotting nectar.

options.desc: 1. Step through the rusted turnstile and enter the neon-drenched park
2. Search the ticket booths for relics of the old world
3. Retreat back toward the fog-shrouded village square
